metadata.description: Fill-in-the-blank benchmark from Callister, Shackelford, Kittel, and Heat Transfer textbooks
metadata.evaluation: String match + LLM-as-judge for partial credit (0-3 scale)
sources: Shackelford - Introduction to Materials Science for Engineers (9th ed)

questions.question: The atomic packing factor (APF) for an FCC crystal structure is ___.
The atomic packing factor (APF) for a BCC crystal structure is ___.
The atomic packing factor (APF) for a simple cubic (SC) structure is ___.
The number of atoms per unit cell in an FCC structure is ___.
The number of atoms per unit cell in a BCC structure is ___.
The coordination number for atoms in an FCC structure is ___.
The coordination number for atoms in a BCC structure is ___.
The ideal c/a ratio for the HCP structure is ___.
In an FCC structure, atoms touch along the face diagonal. The relationship between lattice parameter a and atomic radius r is ___.
The number of distinct Bravais lattices in three dimensions is ___.
The minimum radius ratio (r/R) that can produce a coordination number of 6 in ionic bonding is ___.
The minimum radius ratio (r/R) that can produce a coordination number of 4 in ionic bonding is ___.
The minimum radius ratio (r/R) that can produce a coordination number of 8 is ___.
The minimum radius ratio (r/R) that can produce threefold (CN=3) coordination is ___.
Iron transforms from BCC (ferrite) to FCC (austenite) above ___ °C.
The bond angle in the tetrahedral configuration of covalent bonds with carbon is ___.
The C-C single bond length is ___ nm.
The C-C single bond energy is approximately ___ kJ/mol.
The C=C double bond energy is approximately ___ kJ/mol.
The equilibrium bond length for Na⁺-Cl⁻ in NaCl is ___ nm (using rNa⁺=0.098 nm, rCl⁻=0.181 nm).
The secondary (van der Waals) bond energy for argon is approximately ___ kJ/mol.
The hydrogen bridge (water) secondary bond energy is approximately ___ kJ/mol.
Typical primary bond energies range from approximately ___ to ___ kJ/mol.
Fick's first law states J = ___ (fill in the formula).
The Arrhenius diffusion equation is D = ___.
The maximum solubility of carbon in austenite (FCC iron) is ___ wt%.
The maximum solubility of carbon in ferrite (BCC iron) is ___ wt%.
In Fick's second law, the diffusion penetration depth scales as ___ (in terms of D and t).
The diffusion coefficient of C in γ-Fe at 700°C is approximately 5×10⁻¹² m²/s. After 2 hours, the approximate case depth x ≈ √(Dt) = ___ mm.
Carburization is most effective with two simultaneous conditions: high temperature (increases D) and ___.
questions.ground_truth: sufficient time (for carbon to diffuse to desired depth)

questions.question: The slope of the ln(D) vs 1/T Arrhenius plot equals ___ (in terms of Qd and R).
D₀ (preexponential factor in Arrhenius diffusion) is independent of ___.
Young's modulus E = ___ (in terms of stress σ and strain ε).
Poisson's ratio ν = ___ (in terms of lateral strain and axial strain).
The Hall-Petch equation is σ_y = ___.
According to Hall-Petch, if grain size d is decreased by a factor of 4 (d → d/4), the strengthening term k_y × d^(-1/2) increases by a factor of ___.
The ASTM grain size number G is related to grain diameter d (in mm) by G = ___.
Dislocation density has units of ___.
questions.ground_truth: mm⁻² (per square millimeter) or total length per unit volume

questions.question: For an edge dislocation, the dislocation line motion is ___ to the applied shear stress.
For a screw dislocation, the dislocation line motion is ___ to the applied shear stress.
The three stages of creep are: primary (decreasing rate), secondary (___ rate), and tertiary (accelerating rate).
Fatigue is failure under ___ loading at stress levels below the ultimate tensile strength.
The stress intensity factor at which unstable crack growth occurs is called ___.
questions.ground_truth: fracture toughness K_Ic (plane strain fracture toughness)

questions.question: The longitudinal elastic modulus of a fiber composite by rule of mixtures is Ec = ___.
For E-glass/epoxy (Ef=72 GPa, Em=3 GPa, Vf=0.6), the longitudinal modulus Ec = ___ GPa.
The transverse modulus of a fiber composite follows 1/Ec = ___.
The eutectoid composition in the Fe-C system is ___ wt% C.
The eutectoid temperature in the Fe-C system is ___ °C.
Pearlite is a lamellar mixture of ___ and cementite (Fe₃C).
Cementite (Fe₃C) contains ___ wt% carbon.
Martensite forms by rapid quenching of austenite, trapping carbon in a ___ lattice.
The three steps in precipitation hardening are: solution treatment → rapid quench → ___.
The Jominy end-quench test measures ___ vs distance from the quenched end.
Normalizing steel involves heating above the upper critical temperature and ___ cooling.
The maximum solubility of carbon in austenite occurs at ___ °C.
In the eutectic reaction, one liquid transforms simultaneously into ___ different solid phases.
The band gap of silicon (Si) is ___ eV.
The band gap of germanium (Ge) is ___ eV.
The band gap of gallium arsenide (GaAs) is ___ eV.
The band gap of zinc sulfide (ZnS) is ___ eV.
The band gap of gallium nitride (GaN) is approximately ___ eV.
A semiconductor with Eg = 1.1 eV absorbs photons up to a maximum wavelength of ___ nm (use hc = 1240 eV·nm).
At the Fermi energy E = Ef, the Fermi-Dirac occupation probability f(Ef) = ___ at any temperature T > 0.
questions.source: Callister p.754 / Solid State Physics p.108

questions.question: The electrical resistivity ρ and conductivity σ are related by σ = ___.
Electrical conductivity of metals is typically ~10⁷ S/m; insulators have conductivity < ___ S/m.
The critical temperature Tc of niobium (Nb), the highest among elemental superconductors, is ___ K.
questions.source: Callister p.217 / Solid State Physics p.217

questions.question: In the Shockley-Queisser limit, the maximum theoretical efficiency of a single p-n junction solar cell is approximately ___.
questions.source: Solid State Physics p.166 / Shackelford p.166

questions.question: In n-type doping, the majority carriers are ___.
In p-type doping, the majority carriers are ___.
For ordinary window glass, the composition is approximately ___% silica (SiO₂) by weight.
The resistance of a 100 m copper wire (ρ=1.7×10⁻⁸ Ω·m) with cross-section 1 mm² is ___ Ω.
Thermal conductivity units are ___.
Thermal diffusivity α = ___ (in terms of k, ρ, and cp).
The Fourier number Fo = ___ (dimensionless time in transient conduction).
The Biot number Bi = ___ (ratio of convective to conductive resistance).
The lumped capacitance method is valid when Bi ___ 0.1.
Newton's law of cooling states q = ___.
The Stefan-Boltzmann law for radiation is q = ___.
The error function erf(0) = ___.
Thermal expansion occurs because increasing temperature increases the amplitude of ___, increasing mean atomic spacing.
Stainless steel requires at least ___ wt% chromium to form a protective passive Cr₂O₃ layer.
In a galvanic couple, the ___ metal (more anodic) corrodes preferentially.
Cathodic protection prevents corrosion by making the metal the ___ in an electrochemical cell.
Stress corrosion cracking (SCC) occurs under combined action of ___ stress and a corrosive environment.
In intergranular corrosion of stainless steel, sensitization occurs in the temperature range of ___ °C.
Sintering heats compacted ceramic powder ___ its melting point to bond particles.
Adding ___ wt% MgO to alumina (Lucalox patent) produces a nearly pore-free, translucent ceramic.
The glass transition temperature Tg marks the transition from ___ to glassy behavior in amorphous polymers.
Vulcanization introduces ___ cross-links between rubber chains to improve strength and elasticity.
The most common commercial polymer is ___, with repeat unit -(C₂H₄)-.
The equilibrium number of vacancies follows Nv = N·exp(___ ), where Qv is formation energy and T is temperature.
A Schottky defect consists of a pair of ___ (cation + anion) in an ionic crystal.
A Frenkel defect is an atom displaced from its lattice site to an ___ position.
If vacancy formation energy is 0.9 eV, the vacancy concentration ratio N_v(600K)/N_v(300K) ≈ ___.
The piezoelectric effect is the generation of electric ___ in response to applied mechanical stress.
The Meissner effect is the complete expulsion of ___ from a superconductor when cooled below Tc.
Type II superconductors have two critical fields Hc1 and Hc2. Between them, magnetic flux partially penetrates as quantized ___.
In BCS theory, the energy gap Δ = ___ (in terms of Boltzmann constant k and critical temperature Tc).
In the nearly-free electron model, energy gaps appear at Brillouin zone boundaries k = ___ (in terms of n, π, and lattice parameter a).
The Hall effect is used to determine carrier type, ___, and carrier mobility.
For lightweight structural applications, the key figure of merit is ___ (strength divided by density).
Carbon fiber reinforced polymer (CFRP) is preferred for aerospace structures due to its high ___ (modulus divided by density).
Cemented carbide (WC-Co) maintains high hardness at elevated temperatures because WC has a very high ___.
Yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ) is preferred for thermal barrier coatings because it has very low ___ (~2 W/m·K).
For biomedical implants, the three most critical properties are biocompatibility, ___, and sufficient strength/fatigue resistance.
Bloch's theorem states that the electron wavefunction in a periodic potential takes the form ψk(r) = ___.
questions.ground_truth: uk(r)·e^(ik·r), where uk has the periodicity of the lattice

questions.question: The effective mass of an electron in a band is m* = ___ (in terms of ℏ and the band dispersion d²E/dk²).
In the tight-binding model, the bandwidth equals ___ (in terms of coordination number z and hopping integral t).
The London penetration depth λL is the length scale over which magnetic field ___ exponentially inside a superconductor.
Cooper pairs are pairs of electrons with opposite momenta and spins, bound through ___ interaction.
One mole of solid MgO occupies a cube 22.37 mm on a side. The density of MgO = ___ g/cm³.
The density of copper is approximately ___ g/cm³.
The density of aluminum is approximately ___ g/cm³.
The melting point of copper is ___ °C.
The melting point of tungsten (highest among all metals) is ___ °C.
The melting point of NaCl (ionic solid) is ___ °C.
In solid NaCl, each Na⁺ ion is surrounded by ___ Cl⁻ ions (coordination number).
The reaction energy released per mole during polymerization of ethylene (C=C → 2 C-C) is ___ kJ/mol.
The Si-O bond is approximately ___ % ionic and ___ % covalent in character.
The coordination number for Si⁴⁺ in SiO₂ (radius ratio = 0.039/0.132 = 0.295) is ___.
Cold working increases dislocation density, which ___ yield strength (increases/decreases).
Annealing above the recrystallization temperature causes new ___ grains to nucleate and grow.
In precipitation hardening, the aging step produces fine ___ particles that pin dislocations.
Increasing carbon content in steel from 0.2 to 0.8 wt% significantly ___ yield strength.
Higher sintering temperature for ceramics increases both density and ___.
The diffusion depth in carburization scales as ___ with time at constant temperature.
Grain refinement uniquely increases both strength (via ___) AND toughness.
questions.ground_truth: Hall-Petch relationship (grain boundary blocking of dislocations)

questions.question: For n-type silicon, increasing doping concentration moves the Fermi level ___ the conduction band edge.
Ceramics are brittle because they lack sufficient mobile ___ to relieve stress at crack tips.
The band gap of a semiconductor ___ (increases/decreases) with increasing temperature due to thermal expansion.
Metals have high electrical conductivity because they have ___ valence electrons in partially filled bands.
The electrical resistivity of metals ___ with increasing temperature due to phonon scattering.
FCC iron dissolves more carbon than BCC iron because FCC has larger ___ sites.
Martensite is harder than pearlite because carbon is trapped in a ___ lattice creating high strain.
Cementite (Fe₃C) makes steel harder but more brittle because it ___ dislocation motion.
For case hardening of gears from low-carbon steel, the correct heat treatment sequence is ___ → quench → temper.
For overhead electrical transmission lines, aluminum is preferred over copper despite lower conductivity because of its lower ___.
Galvanic corrosion between aluminum and steel can be prevented by ___ the metals from each other.
For a pressure vessel at elevated temperature under sustained load, the critical failure mode is ___.
Permanent magnets require high ___ (resistance to demagnetization) with sufficient remanence.
The Shockley-Queisser efficiency limit is maximized at a band gap of approximately ___ to ___ eV.
In the quantum Hall effect, Hall conductance is quantized in multiples of ___.
The BCS energy gap relation is Δ = ___ (numerical coefficient × kBTc).
At T=0K in a metal, all states up to the Fermi energy are filled. At finite T, only electrons within ___ of Ef are thermally excited.
The cohesive energy of a solid is strongly correlated with its ___.
In the tight-binding model, wider bandwidth corresponds to ___ hopping integral (orbital overlap).
The Pauli exclusion principle states that no two electrons can occupy the same ___.
questions.ground_truth: quantum state (same set of quantum numbers n, l, ml, ms)

questions.question: The Fermi surface in a metal separates ___ states from unoccupied states at T=0K.
Avogadro's number is ___ atoms per mole (to 4 significant figures).
One atomic mass unit (amu) equals approximately ___ grams.
The six categories of engineering materials in Shackelford are: metals, ceramics, glasses, polymers, composites, and ___.
Kevlar's strength-to-weight ratio is ___ times that of structural steel.
Nylon was invented in ___ at DuPont Company.
About ___ % porosity causes Al₂O₃ to become completely opaque.
About ___ % porosity causes Al₂O₃ to become translucent.
The Al₂O₃ (alumina) Lucalox sodium vapor lamp operates at approximately ___ °C.
Translucent Al₂O₃ lamps provide ___ lumens/W compared to 15 lumens/W for conventional lightbulbs.
The melting point of Al₂O₃ (alumina) is ___ °C.
The melting point of metallic aluminum (Al) is ___ °C.
Electronegativity was defined by ___ (scientist name).
For a MgO-SiO2 ionic pair, the coulombic force Fc = -k₀(Z₁q)(Z₂q)/a². For Si⁴⁺ in SiO₂ with rSi=0.039nm, rO=0.132nm, the bond length a₀ = ___ nm.
The effective C-C bond length projected along the chain axis in polyethylene (bond angle 109.5°) is l = 0.154 × sin(54.75°) = ___ nm.
A polyethylene molecule with n=500 mers has a total extended length L = 500 × 2 × 0.126 nm = ___ nm.
The vacancy concentration increases from 300K to 600K by a factor of exp(17.4) ≈ ___.
If diffusion coefficient doubles from 500°C to 600°C, the approximate activation energy Qd ≈ ___ kJ/mol.
The endurance (fatigue) limit is the stress amplitude below which a material can endure ___ cycles without fatigue failure.
Bainite forms at temperatures ___ (above/between/below) the pearlite and martensite formation ranges in the TTT diagram.
High hardenability in steel means martensite forms at ___ (greater/lesser) depths from the quenched surface.
The material tetrahedron in Shackelford has four corners: structure, properties, processing, and ___.
Fiberglass is a composite of glass fibers embedded in a ___ matrix.
Glass-ceramics are produced by controlled ___ and crystallization of glass through heat treatment.
In ceramics, approximately ___ % (or more) of commercially important ones are chemical compounds of metallic + one of 5 nonmetallic elements (C,N,O,P,S).
The Brinell hardness test uses a hardened steel or ___ ball as indenter.
Residual stresses arise from non-uniform ___ deformation or differential thermal contraction.
True stress-strain curve continues to ___ (rise/fall) until fracture, unlike engineering stress-strain which shows apparent softening.
The phase rule F = C - P + ___ (Gibbs phase rule for a binary system at constant pressure).
questions.ground_truth: 2 (or 1 if pressure is fixed: F = C - P + 1)

questions.question: The lever rule gives the weight fraction of the liquid phase as ___ (in a two-phase region).
questions.ground_truth: (Cα - C₀)/(Cα - CL) or equivalent lever rule formula

questions.question: Intrinsic semiconductor carrier concentration is proportional to ___ (exponential form).
The quantum Hall conductance is quantized in integer multiples of e²/h ≈ ___ S (siemens).
In a metal at finite temperature T, only electrons within ~___ of the Fermi energy are thermally excited.
Dislocations (line defects) are the primary carriers of ___ deformation in crystalline metals.
Solid solution strengthening occurs when alloying elements create ___ fields that impede dislocation motion.
The theoretical APF for close-packed structures (FCC and HCP) is ___.
The Lorentz number relates thermal conductivity to electrical conductivity in metals: k/(σT) = ___ W·Ω/K².
Plane strain fracture toughness K_Ic requires specimen thickness B ≥ ___ (formula involving K_Ic and σ_y).
YAG (yttrium-aluminum-oxide) laser material has the formula ___.
The Iron Age replaced the Bronze Age and defines the period from ___ to 1 BC.
Polyamide (nylon) was the first commercially successful polymer, initially used in toothbrush bristles in ___.
The Coulomb proportionality constant k₀ = ___ V·m/C.
At the eutectoid point (0.76 wt% C, 727°C), austenite transforms to ___ upon slow cooling.
Increasing sintering temperature increases ___ by accelerating diffusion and pore elimination.
The Wiedemann-Franz law states that metals with high electrical conductivity also have high ___ conductivity.
The Glass transition temperature Tg for most common polymers is ___ (above/below) room temperature.
questions.ground_truth: varies; for polyethylene below (−120°C), for polystyrene above (+100°C)

questions.question: Hydrogen embrittlement is the reduction in ductility caused by absorption of ___ hydrogen into the metal lattice.
In the galvanic series, platinum is ___ (most/least) noble and most resistant to corrosion.
Grain boundary diffusion is faster than lattice diffusion because grain boundaries have ___ activation energy.
The diffusivity ordering from fastest to slowest is: ___ > grain boundary > lattice (volume) diffusion.
In FCC iron (austenite), the maximum carbon solubility is 2.14 wt% at ___ °C.
The Nd:YAG laser uses neodymium (Nd) in a ___ matrix to provide the lasing property.
In polyethylene, the weak link that causes softening above ~120°C is the ___ bonding between adjacent molecular chains.
